reference: Hi, as you narrated, head trauma caused by a heavy object or protruding part may lead to a localized depressed fracture of the skull bone, and such a defect may not produce many symptoms. This type of defect may only be present as a dent upon palpation. However, such a fracture usually does not require any treatment or surgery. If you are very concerned, then you may undergo a plain computed tomography scan of the head with a bone window to look for any fracture. I hope you find my advice useful. Wishing you good health.
text: 5. As we narrated, head trauma caused by a heavy object or protruding part may lead to a localized, depressed fracture and disfigurement, and such a defect may not produce any symptoms. This type of defect may only be present as a result of chronic palpation. However, such a fracture usually will not require any treatment or surgery. 6. If you are very concerned, then you may undergo a plain concluded tomography scan of the head with a bone window to look for any fracture. I hope you find my advice useful. Wishing you good health.
